---
title: "Readings"
---

# Course Readings

Readings are organized by day, with **Required** (current, short, do-before-class) and **Recommended** (deeper or historical) tiers.

- Links go to the **source** where it is freely available online.
- **[Find it]** links run a title search (use them if a direct link is paywalled or moves).
- **[Provided]** items are distributed through the course materials — these include copyrighted articles and book excerpts we don't post publicly.

> No textbook purchase is required. *Co-Intelligence* is recommended, not required; its core ideas are captured in Mollick's free essays and newsletter (below).

---

## Pre-Course (Optional but Recommended) {#pre-course}

1. **Mollick, E. (2025).** *I, Cyborg: Using Co-Intelligence.* One Useful Thing. [Read free](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/p/i-cyborg-using-co-intelligence)
   - The free, current stand-in for the book: the jagged frontier, "centaurs vs. cyborgs," and keeping a human in the loop.

2. **World Health Organization. (2024).** *Ethics and governance of AI for health: Guidance on large multi-modal models.* WHO. [Available online](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240084759)
   - The updated, generative-AI-era successor to WHO's 2021 guidance; read the executive summary.

3. **Recommended:** Topol, E. (2019). High-performance medicine. *Nature Medicine, 25*(1), 44--56. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41591-018-0300-7) · "How People Are Really Using AI in 2026." [Provided]

---

## Day 1 — Foundations of AI {#day-1-readings}

### Required

1. **Anthropic. (2024).** *Prompt Engineering Guide.* [Available online](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/build-with-claude/prompt-engineering/overview)
   - Focus on "Be clear and direct" and "Use examples."

2. **Anthropic. (2025).** *Effective context engineering for AI agents.* [Available online](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-context-engineering-for-ai-agents)
   - Why the course now centers *context* engineering over single prompts.

### Recommended

3. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence*, Ch. 1--2. Portfolio/Penguin. [Provided]
4. **Vaswani, A., et al. (2017).** Attention is all you need. *NeurIPS 30.* [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1706.03762) — historical context only.
5. **Governor Shapiro. (2024).** Executive Order on AI governance for the Commonwealth. [Available online](https://www.governor.pa.gov/newsroom/governor-shapiro-signs-executive-order-establishing-governance-guardrails-for-commonwealth-use-of-artificial-intelligence/)
6. **MIT Technology Review. (2026).** *10 Breakthrough Technologies / What matters in AI right now.* [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=MIT+Technology+Review+2026+10+things+that+matter+in+AI) — a quick snapshot of how fast things changed.

---

## Day 2 — Multimodal AI & Working with Models {#day-2-readings}

### Required

1. **Jowsey, T., et al. (2025).** *We reject the use of generative AI for reflexive qualitative research.* [Available online](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/10778004251401851)
   - The current debate on AI in qualitative analysis; pairs with Activity 4.

2. **Boykis, V. (2026).** *Running local models is good now.* [Read free](https://vickiboykis.com/) ([Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Vicki+Boykis+running+local+models+is+good+now)) — open/local models for sensitive data.

### Recommended

3. **OpenDataPhilly. (2024).** Heat Vulnerability Index — Metadata & Documentation. [Available online](https://opendataphilly.org/datasets/heat-vulnerability-by-census-tract/) — review before Activity 3.
4. **Gichoya, J. W., et al. (2022).** AI recognition of patient race in medical imaging. *Lancet Digital Health, 4*(6). [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1016/S2589-7500(22)00063-2)
5. **"Exploring Agent-Assisted Qualitative Analysis."** [Provided] — the constructive counterpoint to Jowsey et al.
6. **Anthropic. (2025).** *Coding agents for data analysis.* [Provided] / [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Anthropic+coding+agents+for+data+analysis)
7. **Meskó, B., & Topol, E. (2023).** Regulatory oversight of LLMs in healthcare. *npj Digital Medicine, 6*. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1038/s41746-023-00873-0) — now largely superseded by WHO (2024).
8. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence*, Ch. 3. [Provided]

---

## Day 3 — Agentic AI, MCP & Loops {#day-3-readings}

### Required

1. **Anthropic. (2025).** *Claude Code Documentation.* [Available online](https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/claude-code) — skim getting-started + capabilities.

2. **Ji, Y. ("Peak"). (2025).** *Context Engineering for AI Agents: Lessons from Building Manus.* [Read free](https://manus.im/blog/Context-Engineering-for-AI-Agents-Lessons-from-Building-Manus)
   - What building a real agent actually takes.

3. **Anthropic / modelcontextprotocol.io.** *Introduction to the Model Context Protocol (MCP).* [Available online](https://modelcontextprotocol.io)

### Recommended

4. **Google. (2025).** *Google AI Studio Documentation.* [Available online](https://ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/ai-studio-quickstart) — review before Activity 4.
5. **Ollama. (2025).** *Ollama Documentation.* [Available online](https://ollama.com/) — for Activity 6.
6. **Wisdom, S. (2025).** *Beyond Vibes: How to Actually Evaluate AI Agents.* Nesta. [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Beyond+Vibes+How+to+Actually+Evaluate+AI+Agents+Nesta)
7. **Forward Future. (2026).** *Loop Library: Repeatable AI Agent Workflows.* [Read free](https://signals.forwardfuture.ai/loop-library/)
8. **Microsoft Research. (2023).** AutoGen: multi-agent LLM applications. [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/2308.08155) — earlier multi-agent framing; included for history.
9. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence*, Ch. 4. [Provided]

---

## Day 4 — AI for Urban Health Workflows {#day-4-readings}

### Required

1. **Mollick, E., & Mollick, L. (2024).** Using AI to implement effective teaching strategies. *Wharton.* [Available online](https://papers.ssrn.com/sol3/papers.cfm?abstract_id=4391243)
   - Human–AI collaboration frameworks that transfer to public-health workflows.

2. **Philadelphia Department of Public Health. (2024).** *Community Health Assessment.* [Available online](https://www.phila.gov/programs/community-health-assessment/) — background for the scenarios.

### Recommended

3. **The Lancet Public Health. (2025).** Artificial intelligence in public health: promises, challenges, and an agenda. [Available online](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanpub/article/PIIS2468-2667(25)00036-2/fulltext) — current field overview.
4. **Anthropic. (2025).** *Coding agents in the social sciences.* [Provided] / [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Anthropic+coding+agents+in+the+social+sciences)
5. **World Economic Forum. (2025).** New Earth-observation tech is boosting climate intelligence. [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=World+Economic+Forum+new+earth+observation+tech+climate+intelligence) — remote sensing for urban health.
6. **Lee, E. C., et al. (2023).** AI for public health surveillance. *Annual Review of Public Health, 44*, 349--373. [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Lee+Artificial+intelligence+for+public+health+surveillance+Annual+Review+2023)
7. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence*, Ch. 5--6. [Provided]

---

## Day 5 — Responsible AI & Future Directions {#day-5-readings}

### Required

1. **Obermeyer, Z., et al. (2019).** Dissecting racial bias in an algorithm used to manage the health of populations. *Science, 366*. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aax2342) — the case in Activity 1.

2. **Mitchell, M., et al. (2019).** Model cards for model reporting. *FAccT.* [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1810.03993)

3. **Gebru, T., et al. (2021).** Datasheets for datasets. *CACM, 64*(12). [Available online](https://arxiv.org/abs/1803.09010)

### Recommended

4. **Court ruling: companies liable for false AI-generated statements (2026).** [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=court+ruled+Google+liable+for+false+statements+generated+by+AI) — accountability when AI errs.
5. **"LLMs can unmask pseudonymous users at scale" (Ars Technica, 2026).** [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=LLMs+can+unmask+pseudonymous+users+at+scale+Ars+Technica) — re-identification / privacy.
6. **"These new tools could make AI vision systems less biased" (MIT Technology Review).** [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=these+new+tools+could+make+AI+vision+systems+less+biased+MIT+Technology+Review)
7. **"What it will take to make AI sustainable" (WIRED).** [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=what+it+will+take+to+make+AI+sustainable+WIRED) — environmental footprint.
8. **Mollick, E. (2025).** *Choosing to Stay Human.* One Useful Thing. [Read free](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/) ([Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Ethan+Mollick+Choosing+to+Stay+Human))
9. **Benjamin, R. (2019).** *Race After Technology*, Ch. 1. [Provided excerpt]
10. **Vayena, E., et al. (2018).** ML in medicine: ethical challenges. *PLoS Medicine.* [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pmed.1002689) · **Char, D. S., et al. (2018).** *NEJM, 378*(11). [Available online](https://doi.org/10.1056/NEJMp1714229)
11. **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence*, Ch. 7--8. [Provided]

---

## Additional Qualitative-Research Readings {#qualitative-readings}

1. **Jowsey, T., et al. (2025).** *We reject the use of generative AI for reflexive qualitative research.* [Available online](https://journals.sagepub.com/doi/10.1177/10778004251401851) (Also Day 2.)
2. **"Exploring Agent-Assisted Qualitative Analysis."** [Provided] — the other side of the debate.
3. **Dai, S. C., et al. (2024).** Using LLMs to generate qualitative interview guides. *JMIR Medical Education, 10*. [Available online](https://doi.org/10.2196/59608)
4. **Guest, G., et al. (2025).** AI-assisted thematic analysis — empirical comparison. [Provided] / [Find it](https://www.google.com/search?q=Guest+2025+AI+thematic+analysis+comparison)
5. **Jowsey, T., et al. (2024).** AI-assisted thematic analysis (commentary). *Medical Education, 58*(11). [Find it](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=Jowsey+AI-assisted+thematic+analysis+Medical+Education+2024)

---

## Recommended Resources {#recommended-resources}

### Current field overviews (AI + public health)

- **The Lancet Public Health (2025).** AI in public health: promises, challenges, and an agenda. [Link](https://www.thelancet.com/journals/lanpub/article/PIIS2468-2667(25)00036-2/fulltext)
- **ASPPH (2025).** *Harnessing Innovation: AI for Public Health* (roadmap for academic public health). [Link](https://aspph.org/initiatives/ai-for-public-health/)
- **WHO (2024).** Guidance on large multi-modal models. [Link](https://www.who.int/publications/i/item/9789240084759)

### Books

- **Mollick, E. (2024).** *Co-Intelligence: Living and Working with AI.* Portfolio/Penguin. — recommended (not required; free essays above cover the core ideas).
- **Benjamin, R. (2019).** *Race After Technology.* Polity.
- **O'Neil, C. (2016).** *Weapons of Math Destruction.* Crown.
- **Topol, E. (2019).** *Deep Medicine.* Basic Books.

### Newsletters

- **Ethan Mollick — [One Useful Thing](https://www.oneusefulthing.org/)** · **The Batch — [deeplearning.ai](https://www.deeplearning.ai/the-batch/)** · **Import AI — [Jack Clark](https://importai.substack.com/)**

### Online Courses

- **Google.** [Intro to Generative AI](https://cloud.google.com/learn/training/machinelearning-AI) — free, self-paced.
- **Coursera.** [AI for Everyone](https://www.coursera.org/learn/ai-for-everyone) — non-technical.

### Organizations

- **[Partnership on AI](https://partnershiponai.org/)** · **[AI Now Institute](https://ainowinstitute.org/)** · **[Stanford HAI](https://hai.stanford.edu/)**
